Title: Adaptation of hydroxymethylbutenyl diphosphate reductase enables volatile isoprenoid production

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: HDR/ispH (panel of heterologous plant, cyanobacterial and bacterial HDR genes; including RcHDR, PtHDR1, PpHDR, EgHDR1, TcHDR, AtHDR, EgHDR, PsHDR1, PsHDR2, Synechococcus HDR, and E. coli ispH)
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: E. coli ispH Gene_ID:944777; EcoGene:EG11081; ECK0030; lytB; Ricinus communis XP_002519102.1 / plasmid MH605331; Populus trichocarpa HDR1 ACD70402 / plasmid MH605329; Populus trichocarpa HDR2 PNT41333.1 / plasmid MH605330; Prunus persica XP_007199828.1 / plasmid MH605326; Eucalyptus grandis HDR1 XP_010028563.1 / plasmid MH605323; Eucalyptus grandis HDR2 XP_010047332.1 / plasmid MH605324; Theobroma cacao XP_007042717.1 / plasmid MH605333; Arabidopsis thaliana AEE86362.1 / plasmid MH605322; Elaeis guineensis XP_010909277.1 / plasmid MH605325; Picea sitchensis HDR1 ACN40284.1 / plasmid MH605327; Picea sitchensis HDR2 ACN39959.1 / plasmid MH605328; Synechococcus sp. PCC 7002 ACA98524.1 / plasmid MH605332
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple species: Escherichia coli; Ricinus communis; Populus trichocarpa; Prunus persica; Eucalyptus grandis; Theobroma cacao; Arabidopsis thaliana; Elaeis guineensis; Picea sitchensis; Synechococcus sp. PCC 7002
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Escherichia coli; Ricinus communis; Populus trichocarpa; Prunus persica; Eucalyptus grandis; Theobroma cacao; Arabidopsis thaliana; Elaeis guineensis; Picea sitchensis; Synechococcus sp. PCC 7002
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered E. coli under IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxymethylbutenyl diphosphate reductase; IspH/HDR; terminal enzyme of plastidic/bacterial MEP pathway; [4Fe-4S] reductase family
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Native plastids/chloroplasts in plants; cytosol of engineered E. coli after truncation of transit peptides
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step controlling DMAPP versus IPP output for downstream short-chain and long-chain isoprenoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/junction for volatile versus non-volatile isoprenoid allocation; not described as universal rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dimethylallyl diphosphate (DMAPP) and isopentenyl diphosphate (IPP) in variable ratios
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBPP to the two prenyl diphosphate isomers IPP and DMAPP, with enzyme-dependent product ratios
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct precursor pool and determines relative availability of DMAPP versus IPP for volatile isoprenoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant volatile organic compounds affecting biosphere-atmosphere interactions and useful for industrial biofuels, fragrances and pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile/emitted from plant tissues; measured as isoprene released from engineered E. coli headspace
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Different HDR enzymes generated up to ~40-fold different DMAPP:IPP ratios; high-DMAPP-producing HDRs enabled greater isoprene production, whereas IPP-biased HDRs favored non-volatile/longer-chain isoprenoid precursor balance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional complementation of lethal E. coli ΔispH knockout; heterologous overexpression; LC-MS/MS quantification of DMAPP and IPP; isoprene production assay; growth-rate toxicity analysis; LC-MS proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered E. coli with DXS overexpression and either lycopene or isoprene sink; IPTG induction; 30–37°C culture conditions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IDI; DXS; IspS; lycopene pathway enzymes CrtE; CrtI; CrtB
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolomics for IPP/DMAPP; LC-MS proteomics; source data provided in paper, no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDR product ratio is not fixed across species but varies widely toward either DMAPP or IPP. 2. HDRs from volatile isoprenoid-emitting species generally produce more DMAPP and support higher isoprene formation in engineered E. coli. 3. HDR acts as a metabolic junction shaping carbon allocation between volatile short-chain and non-volatile isoprenoids. 4. Gymnosperm paralogs can be functionally specialized, as shown by contrasting PsHDR1 and PsHDR2 activities.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selection of HDR variant is important in metabolic engineering of isoprenoids to match desired DMAPP:IPP demand; high-DMAPP HDRs may improve isoprene/short-chain terpenoid production, but balancing flux and avoiding prenyl-phosphate toxicity is critical
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Certain HDR overexpression constructs caused severe dose-dependent growth defects and DMAPP/prenyl-phosphate toxicity in E. coli; very high DMAPP-producing PsHDR2 reduced growth rate by ~50%
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s core mechanistic subject is HDR adaptation across species rather than DXS, IDI, IspS or carotenoid enzymes, which were supporting engineering modules. Product ratios measured in vivo are influenced by downstream sink context but relative differences between HDRs remained consistent.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxymethylbutenyl diphosphate reductase; gymnosperm Type I HDR
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Native plastid-targeted protein; expressed in E. coli cytosol after transit peptide truncation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal MEP step controlling DMAPP/IPP precursor partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control determinant for precursor ratio in this study
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Primarily IPP with lower DMAPP output
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts HMBPP into IPP and DMAPP, with product ratio shifted toward IPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; disfavors DMAPP-rich volatile isoprenoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile isoprenoid relevant to atmospheric interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PsHDR1 produced the most IPP-biased ratio among tested HDRs and supported very low isoprene production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; LC-MS/MS IPP/DMAPP quantification; isoprene assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with PtDXS and either lycopene pathway or isoprene synthase sink; IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PtDXS; IspS; IDI; CrtE; CrtI; CrtB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsHDR1 generated a DMAPP:IPP ratio of about 0.447 ± 0.19, strongly biased toward IPP. 2. PsHDR1 enabled much lower isoprene production than PsHDR2. 3. PsHDR1 likely represents a gymnosperm Type I paralog specialized for basal/long-chain isoprenoid precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely unsuitable for engineering high isoprene output but may be useful where more IPP-balanced flux is desired
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low support for isoprene formation; may limit short-chain volatile terpene production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes PsHDR1 as a functionally specialized paralog distinct from PsHDR2.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxymethylbutenyl diphosphate reductase; gymnosperm Type II HDR
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Native plastid-targeted protein; expressed in E. coli cytosol after transit peptide truncation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal MEP step controlling DMAPP/IPP precursor partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control determinant for precursor ratio in this study
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Primarily DMAPP with >10-fold excess over IPP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts HMBPP into IPP and DMAPP, with product ratio shifted strongly toward DMAPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct DMAPP-rich precursor pool favoring short-chain volatile isoprenoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile isoprenoid relevant to atmospheric interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PsHDR2 produced >10-fold more DMAPP than IPP and enabled higher isoprene production than PsHDR1, but caused strong growth toxicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; complementation testing; LC-MS/MS IPP/DMAPP quantification; isoprene assay; growth-rate analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with PtDXS and lycopene or isoprene sink; IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PtDXS; IspS; IDI; CrtE; CrtI; CrtB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsHDR2 is a high-DMAPP-producing HDR, opposite to PsHDR1. 2. PsHDR2 supports substantially higher isoprene production than PsHDR1. 3. PsHDR2 overexpression is highly toxic in E. coli, reducing growth by about 50%. 4. PsHDR2 and PsHDR1 provide strong evidence of gymnosperm HDR paralog specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising for engineering isoprene or other DMAPP-demanding short-chain isoprenoids, but requires toxicity management and flux balancing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High prenyl-phosphate/DMAPP-associated toxicity; initial complementation failed likely because no sink was present; reduced host growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study highlights PsHDR2 as evidence that gymnosperms may deploy separate HDR paralogs for different isoprenoid demands.
